spec_match.rationale: Captures the screen twice — once with default settings, once via `applyConstraints` using the new screenPixelRatio info to back off to logical pixels — and demonstrates the `dpr²` pixel-per-frame reduction. That is the explainer's motivating example.

interactive.rationale: Working start-capture buttons (with 5 listeners) drive both capture variants A and B with live measurements of pixels-per-frame, side by side. Feature-detect probe at top.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index says `capture twice — once at default, once at logical resolution via applyConstraints() — and watch the pixels-per-frame drop by dpr²`. Page is exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: The two-capture comparison covers the win case but doesn't handle the 1x display branch where there's nothing to save, nor the case where `applyConstraints` is unsupported and silently no-ops.

references.rationale: `see also` cites the chromestatus link and the mediacapture-screen-share screenPixelRatio anchor.

design_system.rationale: 48 CSS variable references, no raw hex, no button override mismatch.

openQuestions.title: Show the 1x-display short-circuit
openQuestions.detail: On a 1x monitor the optimisation is a no-op (saves zero). Surface that branch explicitly so authors don't deploy code that wastes a call to applyConstraints.

openQuestions.title: Add a bitrate estimation readout
openQuestions.detail: Pixels-per-frame is shown but most readers care about Mbps savings. Multiply by an assumed bits-per-pixel (e.g. H.264's ~0.1 bpp at the chosen quality) and surface the estimated upstream bandwidth savings so the demo speaks the language of VC apps.

openQuestions.title: Cover the constraints-not-supported branch
openQuestions.detail: If a browser doesn't honour the screenPixelRatio constraint, `applyConstraints` may resolve without changing anything. Add a post-apply check (`track.getSettings().screenPixelRatio` after vs before) and flag silent no-ops.

summary: Excellent quantitative demo of the explainer's motivating bandwidth savings with a real A/B capture comparison and proper references. Edge coverage of the 1x-display short-circuit and the silent-no-op constraint case would round it out, and a Mbps estimate would translate pixels-per-frame into the metric VC engineers actually optimise for.
